acceptance_criteria.title: Fix C — BASH_MAX_CONSECUTIVE updated to 5 in all 4 locations
acceptance_criteria.given: Fix C has been applied to hooks/pretool-orchestrator-gate.py, CLAUDE.md, and scripts/execute-push.py
acceptance_criteria.when: the 3 implementation files are grep-inspected for old and new limit values
acceptance_criteria.then: grep for 'BASH_MAX_CONSECUTIVE = 3' in the 3 files returns zero results; grep for 'BASH_MAX_CONSECUTIVE = 5' in gate.py returns exactly one result; grep for 'capped at 3' in gate.py returns zero results (docstring lines 6-7 updated; note docstring is line-wrapped so grep targets the shorter pattern 'capped at 3'); grep for 'Bash capped at 3 consecutive' in CLAUDE.md returns zero results and grep for '5 consecutive' in CLAUDE.md returns at least one result; grep for '3-consecutive-Bash' or '3 consecutive' in execute-push.py returns zero results

assertions.description: BASH_MAX_CONSECUTIVE = 5 present exactly once in gate.py
No 'Bash capped at 3 consecutive' in CLAUDE.md

acceptance_criteria.title: Fix A — Step 0 inserted in close.md before Normal-path artifact preflight
acceptance_criteria.given: Fix A has been applied to commands/close.md
acceptance_criteria.when: commands/close.md is read and searched for the Step 0 section
acceptance_criteria.then: a '### Step 0 (Parallel-Dev Only)' section appears BEFORE the '### Normal-path artifact preflight' section; Step 0 contains Case 1 logic (canonical absent + 2+ task-scoped shards → invoke scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id $TASK_ID); Step 0 contains Case 2 logic (canonical present + 2+ task-scoped shards → validate via script, record parallel_aggregate_written); Step 0 contains Case 3 logic (1 or fewer shards → skip Step 0); shard detection uses both naming patterns from hooks/pretool-aggregate-check.py scoped to TASK_ID; the result of the script is captured so Step 1 can read parallel_aggregate_written

assertions.description: Step 0 appears before Normal-path artifact preflight

acceptance_criteria.title: Fix D — Step 1 inspector dispatch serializes when parallel cycle detected
acceptance_criteria.given: Fix D has been applied to commands/close.md Step 1
acceptance_criteria.then: the Step 1 heading no longer reads 'three inspectors in parallel' unconditionally; the dispatch instruction contains a conditional: when parallel_aggregate_written=true OR dev-report.parallel_workers is non-empty OR 2+ task-scoped shards detected → dispatch inspectors sequentially; when none of the above conditions hold → dispatch inspectors in parallel (original behavior preserved)

assertions.description: Step 1 heading no longer unconditionally says 'three inspectors in parallel'
assertions.pattern: Step 1: Agent dispatch — three inspectors in parallel

acceptance_criteria.title: Fix B_contract — Structured status output section in changelog-analyst.md with corrected git commands
acceptance_criteria.given: Fix B_contract has been applied to agents/changelog-analyst.md
acceptance_criteria.when: agents/changelog-analyst.md is read and searched for the structured status section
acceptance_criteria.then: a '## Structured Final Status Output' section exists; it defines commit_status in {committed, nothing_to_commit, nothing_to_commit_precommitted, failed}; it defines auto_bulk_commits[] (array of {repo_root, branch, sha}) present only when status=nothing_to_commit_precommitted; it defines failure_reason + failure_code fields present only when status=failed; the trigger condition uses HEAD_SHA=$(git rev-parse --verify HEAD) + git show -s --format=%s for subject check + git show --name-only --format= for file list (blank lines filtered); the section does NOT use 'git show --name-only --format= HEAD' for commit message matching

assertions.description: Structured Final Status Output section exists
SHA-stable git show -s --format=%s used for subject check
git rev-parse --verify HEAD used for SHA capture

acceptance_criteria.title: Fix P5 — Retry protocol in commit.md with explicit failure_code enum
acceptance_criteria.given: Fix P5 has been applied to commands/commit.md
acceptance_criteria.then: a retry protocol section exists after the changelog-analyst dispatch (Step 6); the protocol defines retryable failure_codes as grant_missing, grant_expired, grant_consumed; the protocol defines non-retryable codes including git_error, staging_error, hook_blocked; the protocol handles status=nothing_to_commit_precommitted → record auto_bulk_commits[] in Step 7 summary; the protocol handles status=nothing_to_commit → warn user, do not treat as error; grant revocation uses a named script (scripts/write-commit-grant.py --revoke-existing-for-task or a named helper), NOT inline shell glob/rm

assertions.description: Retry protocol section exists after Step 6
No inline find/rm for grant revocation
assertions.note: grant revocation must use named script

acceptance_criteria.title: New script — scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py exists and is correct
acceptance_criteria.when: the script is read and inspected
acceptance_criteria.then: the script uses argparse with --task-id required argument; scans docs/dev/ using BOTH regex patterns PER_WORKER_ROLE_FIRST_RE and PER_WORKER_TASK_FIRST_RE from hooks/pretool-aggregate-check.py; applies NON_WORKER_LABELS frozenset and NON_WORKER_LABEL_RE exactly as in pretool-aggregate-check.py (no invented labels); validates that all found shards agree on task_id, baseline_head_sha, baseline_dirty_snapshot, and have dev.status==completed; writes docs/dev/dev-report-<task-id>.json with all fields from commands/dev.md:729-745 PLUS top-level task_id field; exits 0 on success, non-zero with descriptive stderr on failure; contains no hardcoded absolute paths; contains no inline python3 -c or bash -c sub-processes

acceptance_criteria.title: commands/dev.md inline-script instruction removed
acceptance_criteria.given: Fix M7 has been applied to commands/dev.md
acceptance_criteria.when: commands/dev.md is read and searched for the old inline-aggregate instruction
acceptance_criteria.then: the line with 'do NOT add a separate script' is absent; the lines with 'orchestrator writes inline via jq or Python in a single Bash call — no separate script' are absent; the Step 9 section contains an instruction to invoke scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id $TASK_ID (or equivalent referencing the named script)

assertions.description: Old 'do NOT add a separate script' instruction removed
assertions.pattern: do NOT add a separate script
orchestrator writes inline via jq or Python in a single Bash call

acceptance_criteria.title: No new hardcoded paths introduced in any modified file
acceptance_criteria.given: all modified files are diff-inspected after Fix C, Fix A+D, Fix B_contract+P5, and the new script are applied
acceptance_criteria.when: searching for hardcoded absolute paths in the new/modified logic sections of each changed file
acceptance_criteria.then: no new hardcoded absolute paths (e.g., /root/..., /dev/shm/..., /tmp/claude-...) appear in the new/modified logic sections; any path construction in new code derives from environment variables, argparse inputs, or project-root-relative calculation

assertions.description: No new /root/ hardcoded paths in diff
No new /dev/shm/ hardcoded paths in diff
